channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Double yellows needed on Walden Road opposite downs avenue, 19th April
item.description:
The parking opposite downs Avenue on Walden Road makes it difficult to safely get in and out of the turning due to parked cars. Please can double yellows be put opposite this turning on Walden Road to avoid this. Also a hazard when driving down Walden Road towards the roundabout as the parking on the left causes you to drive on opposite side of road and cars fly round the corner from the roundabout risking head on collision.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Walden Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Clothes and suitcase fly tipped, 19th April
item.description:
Road looks absolutely filthy. Plus people have fly tipped clothing and suitcases on the street which are a hazard.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Newick Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 19th April
item.description:
I am writing as a local resident to raise a concern regarding items that were left on the public pavement outside the property at 145–147 Swingate Lane, owned by Mr Kultar Vagha, on Sunday morning. The items were placed there by an individual who I observed entering and exiting the building, which comprises multiple flats and commercial units. These materials were left in a visible and exposed position, detracting from the appearance of the area and potentially presenting a health and safety concern to pedestrians. I subsequently contacted Mr Vagha, who acknowledged that the items had been placed outside by a tenant and indicated that the tenant had arranged a collection directly with the council. However, I have concerns regarding this explanation. It is my understanding that leaving items on the public pavement in this manner is generally subject to control, and I would be grateful if you could confirm whether such arrangements are typically authorised, particularly in cases involving larger properties where designated waste storage arrangements would normally be expected. I would also welcome clarification as to whether it is considered acceptable practice for tenants of privately rented properties to leave items on the public pavement in this way.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Swingate Lane, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Total resurfacing needed now, 19th April
item.description:
Fixing potholes isn’t good enough. Petts Wood Road needs resurfacing. Sending details to a contractor is not working. No repair work is being undertaken. Why not? Many people have raised this serious issue but nothing gets done. Action needs to be taken.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 76, Petts Wood Road, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking drive double yellow blips, 19th April
item.description:
Every day between 20:30 and 23:00
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Motorbike being ridden around Elmstead wood, 19th April
item.description:
Motorbike being ridden along footpath within Elmstead wood causing a hazard for pedestrians, children, animals and wildlife.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beaconsfield Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste accumulation, unsafe ground and fox burrowing in communal parking area, 19th April
item.description:
There is ongoing waste accumulation beneath hedge lines in the communal parking area. There are also large holes and unstable ground creating a safety risk. Fox burrowing activity is present and contributing to the ground damage. This is a combined

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Northiam Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A black bag, a plastic box and a mental frame left outside house, 19th April
item.description:
A pile of rubbish has been left on the pavement outside 72 Pembroke Road. This has been an issue before but residents put rubbish in their bins (landlord aware), however it would appear the bins are also being filled up with other people’s rubbish (not residents) and now fly tippers are starting to leave stuff on the pavement as bins full.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pembroke Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped rubbish, 19th April
item.description:
Rubbish dumped last week, please kindly clear.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Warneford Street, Hackney, London

item.title: Brown waste bins and cardboard, 19th April
item.description:
3 brown bins on pavement. Unclear which property is they belong to Cardboards TV box on pavements

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Foulden Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Fly tipping on Zangwill Road, 19th April
item.description:
I am reporting fly tipping that has just happened in the past hour. They have taken down the penalty fine sign and sofas have been dumped between the two transit vans that I also reported today. We need new penalty signs fixed and cameras to stop these parasites doing this.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Zangwill Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A single mattress was dumped in alleyway., 19th April
item.description:
A single mattress was abandoned in the alleyway, today 19th April 2016. The mattress is blocking the way for cars, people or a fire engine in case of fire.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 161, Green Lane, Chislehurst, Greater London

item.title: Flytipping . Many items, 19th April
item.description:
Flytipping. Suitcases, carpets, broken furniture and other rubbish. Please could the rubbish be removed. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Eltham Palace Road, Eltham
